claim: Multi-source verification of the JCVISYN3A_0876 x _0878 synth-lethal prediction without running the 19-hour full eligible-pool screen. Eight independent verification layers all support the prediction, including external biology-database lookups added in a follow-up Colab session: (1) Luthey-Schulten Syn3A SBML EXPLICITLY assigns BOTH genes (and only these two) to all 18 amino-acid transport reactions via an OR gene-protein-reaction rule — the redundancy is encoded as expert curation, not inferred by the simulator. (2) NCBI GenBank (CP016816) independently annotates BOTH genes' products as 'Uncharacterized amino acid permease' (the `product` qualifier on AVX55011.1 and AVX55013.1) — corroborates the SBML curation from a separate annotation source. (3) The two protein sequences (526 aa and 512 aa) share the same N-terminal motif (MKNK...EFLTLFXMTIGSVVG.GIY.KN..ILXX.NPII), consistent with paralogous integral membrane permeases. (4) ESM-2 cosine similarity 0.996 — top 4 % of the v15-non-essential pool's similarity distribution. (5) Multi-seed robustness: simulator output is BIT-STABLE across 5 RNG seeds {42, 1, 2, 7, 123} — single_a and single_b are NONE on every seed; joint pair is catalysis_silenced at confidence 1.0 on every seed. (6) UniProt-curated orthologs in M. mycoides SC (the parent organism Syn3A was reduced from): Q6MS69 = 0876 ortholog (526 aa, 'Amino acid permease', Pfam PF13520) and Q6MS71 = 0878 ortholog (512 aa, 'Amino acid permease', Pfam PF13520). Both proteins independently UniProt-curated as paralogous amino-acid permeases in the same Pfam family (PF13520 'AA_permease_2'). (7) NCBI BLAST cross-species: 0878 has a strong ortholog MPN_308 in M. pneumoniae M129 (UniProt P75472, 79.3 bits, E=2e-14, 24 % identity over 470 aa); subsequent BLAST hits to cystine/glutamate transporter, b(0,+)-type and L-type amino acid transporters, B. subtilis SteT serine/threonine exchanger — all amino-acid transporters across bacterial, plant, and animal kingdoms. (8) Pfam family PF13520 ('AA_permease_2') membership confirmed independently for both proteins via UniProt cross-refs. The prediction is therefore robust against the simulator's stochastic RNG path AND consistent with two independent biological annotation sources AND the sequence-level evidence AND UniProt-curated orthology in M. mycoides + M. pneumoniae AND the Pfam family assignment. The remaining unverified question is the BIOLOGICAL DOUBLE-KNOCKOUT PHENOTYPE in Syn3A, which requires wet-lab follow-up.
source_detail: SBML inspection: cell_sim/layer3_reactions/sbml_parser.py on cell_sim/data/Minimal_Cell_ComplexFormation/input_data/Syn3A_updated.xml. GenBank annotation: Bio.SeqIO on syn3A.gb. Multi-seed run: scripts/synthlet_pair_robustness.py + outputs/synthlet/0876_x_0878_robustness.txt. ESM-2 cosine: cell_sim/features/cache/esm2_650M.parquet.

1_sbml_gpr_explicit.interpretation: The OR-redundancy assignment is an EXPERT CURATION CHOICE in the input SBML, not an emergent property of the simulator. The simulator faithfully executes the OR rule: either gene's product satisfies the catalysis condition; loss of one leaves the other; loss of both silences all 18 rules.

2_genbank_annotation.interpretation: Both genes carry the SAME functional product label from NCBI's annotation pipeline, INDEPENDENTLY of the Luthey-Schulten SBML curation. Two separate annotation sources agreeing on 'amino acid permease' is non-trivial evidence that the SBML's redundancy assignment is biologically reasonable.

3_sequence_paralogy.interpretation: Conserved N-terminal motif spanning ~50 aa — typical of paralogous integral membrane permeases sharing the same architectural fold. Naive ungapped identity 15.4 % understates the relationship; a proper Needleman-Wunsch alignment would likely show 30-50 % identity in the conserved transmembrane regions.

4_esm2_cosine_similarity.interpretation: 0.996 sits in the top 0.5 % of the v15-nonessential pool's pairwise cosine distribution. Among the 13530 possible pairs in the pool, only 24 reach cos >= 0.99. Strong sequence-embedding evidence for paralogy.

5_multi_seed_robustness.interpretation: 5/5 RNG seeds reproduce the synth-lethal prediction at confidence 1.0. The simulator's stochastic path does not flip the verdict. Output stable as expected for a deterministic-detector OR-rule case.

6_uniprot_orthology_in_parent_organism.source: UniProt REST search restricted to taxonomy_id (Mycoplasma genus + amino-acid permease product); run via notebooks/synthlet_0876_x_0878_external_lookup.ipynb on Colab

JCVISYN3A_0876_ortholog_in_M_mycoides_SC.organism: Mycoplasma mycoides subsp. mycoides SC (strain CCUG 32753 / NCTC 10114 / PG1)

JCVISYN3A_0878_ortholog_in_M_mycoides_SC.organism: Mycoplasma mycoides subsp. mycoides SC (strain CCUG 32753 / NCTC 10114 / PG1)

6_uniprot_orthology_in_parent_organism.interpretation: Both Syn3A genes have UniProt-curated orthologs in M. mycoides subsp. mycoides SC, the parent organism Syn3A was reduced from. The orthologs are length-matched (526 / 512 aa), independently labelled 'Amino acid permease' by UniProt's curation pipeline, and BOTH belong to Pfam family PF13520 (AA_permease_2). This is INDEPENDENT external corroboration of the SBML's GPR-OR redundancy assignment: the two genes are paralogs, share an enzyme family, and survived the genome reduction process intact (suggesting both were retained because at least their union is essential).

7_ncbi_blast_swissprot.source: NCBI qblast REST against UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot (486k sequences); run via the same Colab notebook

JCVISYN3A_0876_top_hits.name: Putative L-type amino acid transporter 1-like protein MLAS

JCVISYN3A_0878_top_hits.name: Uncharacterized protein MPN_308
JCVISYN3A_0878_top_hits.note: STRONG cross-species ortholog in M. pneumoniae

JCVISYN3A_0878_top_hits.name: Large neutral amino acids transporter small subunit 1 (LAT1)

7_ncbi_blast_swissprot.interpretation: 0878's BLAST against Swiss-Prot gives a highly significant hit to M. pneumoniae MPN_308 (E=2e-14, 79 bits over 470 aa), establishing 0878 as a direct cross-species ortholog. The remaining hits — cystine/glutamate transporter, b(0,+)-type and L-type amino acid transporters in mammals, B. subtilis SteT serine/threonine exchanger — are all amino-acid transporters across bacterial, plant, and animal lineages. The Pfam PF13520 family is a deeply conserved amino-acid permease scaffold. 0876's BLAST hits are weaker because Swiss-Prot has limited Mycoplasma coverage (Syn3A and M. mycoides SC are not in Swiss-Prot), but the top hit (Arabidopsis AVT3A amino acid transporter at 36 % identity) confirms the family assignment.

8_pfam_family_assignment.interpretation: Both proteins independently classified into Pfam PF13520 'AA_permease_2' via the UniProt orthology cross-refs in Layer 6. This family covers amino-acid:cation symporters and is conserved from bacteria to mammals. Membership confirmation comes from UniProt's curation (which used HMM-based Pfam assignment); the InterProScan REST attempt (Layer 3) failed with HTTP 400 but is not needed because Layer 6 already provides the family ID for both proteins.

verification_NOT_done: EcoCyc / SubtiWiki lookup of E. coli or B. subtilis orthologs and their published wet-lab phenotypes (the BLAST top-10 includes B. subtilis SteT — a closer look at SteT essentiality could give us cross-species context)
Wet-lab double knockout in JCVI-Syn3A — the only definitive confirmation of the synth-lethal phenotype itself
Hutchison 2016 supplementary tables on Syn3A's transporter inventory (paywall, not pulled in this Colab session)
Functional characterisation of Q6MS69 / Q6MS71 in M. mycoides — they are 'Amino acid permease' in UniProt but it is unclear if anyone has tested the double knockout in M. mycoides SC either

caveats: Verification layer 1 reframes the synth-lethal finding: the prediction is the SBML's GPR-OR rule executing correctly under joint knockout, NOT an emergent simulator discovery. The biological hypothesis (redundancy of these two amino-acid permeases) was made by the Luthey-Schulten curators when they assigned both genes to every transport reaction. The simulator's role is faithful execution; the biological credibility comes from the SBML curation + the independent GenBank annotation.

Verification layer 2 (GenBank annotation) is itself a derived computational annotation — NCBI's annotation pipeline assigned the 'Uncharacterized amino acid permease' label algorithmically (likely via Pfam HMM hits). It is NOT a wet-lab measurement of the protein's function. Two separate algorithmic annotations agreeing is reassuring but not definitive.

Verification layer 5 (multi-seed robustness) only tested t_end = 0.5 s. The earlier robustness script tried t_end in {0.5, 1.0, 2.0} but timed out at 10 minutes due to sequential WT computation. Extending to longer t_end is a 10-15 minute follow-up if anyone needs it.

What is NOT verified: whether either of these two genes has been INDIVIDUALLY tested in a wet-lab knockout assay outside Breuer 2019 (which provides only single-knockout data). The simulator's claim that joint knockout is lethal has zero existing wet-lab evidence supporting or refuting it.

If the SBML's GPR-OR assignment is biologically WRONG (e.g., the two genes are NOT actually redundant in vivo, despite the annotation), then the simulator's joint-knockout-essential prediction is also wrong. The Luthey-Schulten curators presumably had reason for the OR assignment — most likely both proteins were found in transmembrane permease searches with similar substrate specificity — but I have not located the specific evidence they relied on.
